dedication: [Optional: Dedicate to community members, knowledge keepers, or family members who contributed their knowledge]
acknowledgments:
This Heritage Book was created with respect for the knowledge holders who shared their traditions. [Add specific acknowledgments here.]

We acknowledge that the knowledge documented in this book belongs to the communities and individuals who shared it. Our role as documenters is to be faithful stewards of their words and ways.
territorialAcknowledgment: This work was conducted on the traditional territory of the [NATION_NAME] people. We acknowledge their ongoing relationship with [TERRITORY_NAME], and we express our gratitude for the opportunity to learn from and document knowledge within their traditional lands. [If the work crosses multiple territories, name each nation separately.] We recognize that [NATION_NAME] people have never ceded their sovereignty over their lands, knowledge, or cultural practices.
culturalSovereigntyStatement: The traditional knowledge documented in this Heritage Book belongs to the communities and individuals who shared it. This book is presented as an educational resource, not as a claim of ownership over any cultural practice, language, or knowledge system. First Nations content in this book is presented in accordance with OCAP® principles (Ownership, Control, Access, Possession) — the intellectual property of the First Nations Information Governance Centre (FNIGC). Inuit content is presented in accordance with the National Inuit Strategy on Research (NISR) framework. All knowledge holders named in this book have provided documented consent for their contributions. Users of this book are directed to seek deeper knowledge directly from the source communities and to support community-based cultural programs.
